message.content:
Design the implementation plan for the Rearden demo web app — a video-first hiring platform.

## Current Codebase State

**Monorepo structure (Turborepo):**
- `apps/web` — Vite + React 19 + TypeScript + SCSS Modules + Motion v12 (port 3000)
- `apps/api` — Hono + Node.js + Prisma 7 + SQLite (port 3001)
- `packages/types` — shared TS types (User, Candidate, Recruiter, ApiResponse)
- `packages/tsconfig` — shared TS configs

**Existing types:**
```ts
interface User { id, email, name, role: "candidate"|"recruiter", createdAt, updatedAt }
interface Candidate extends User { role: "candidate", resume: string|null, skills: string[], experience: number }
interface Recruiter extends User { role: "recruiter", company, position }
interface ApiResponse<T> { success, data?, error?, meta? }
```

**Web app currently:** Just a placeholder with "Rearden" heading and demo user JSON.
**API currently:** Just `/` and `/health` routes.

## Requirements

### Pages (React Router):
1. **Landing page** (`/`) — hero section, CTA buttons for candidates and recruiters
2. **Candidate registration** (`/register`) — form: name, role, skills, location, video upload
3. **Candidate profile** (`/candidate/:id`) — public profile with video player, skills, info
4. **Recruiter search** (`/search`) — THE MAIN PAGE. AI-powered search with candidate cards + video previews

### Candidate side:
- Registration form with video upload
- Profile page with real video playback

### Recruiter side (MAIN FOCUS):
- Search page with smart search bar
- Candidate cards with thumbnail/preview
- Click through to full profile with video
- AI search uses smart mock (fuzzy matching on skills/keywords with simulated delay, no real Claude API)

### Video handling:
- Real video upload through the profile form
- Store videos as files on disk via API (uploads directory)
- API endpoints: POST /upload (multipart), GET /uploads/:filename (serve files)
- Real video playback in candidate profiles and search feed
- For the 10+ mock candidates: use free stock video URLs from Pexels/Pixabay CDN

### Design:
- Dark theme — premium, unique feel (NOT generic Tailwind/Bootstrap)
- Smooth Motion animations on page transitions, card appearances, hover states
- Mobile responsive
- Focus on making the recruiter search experience impressive for investors

### Data:
- 10+ diverse mock candidates with real stock video URLs, varied skills, names, locations
- Mock data stored in a module (no real DB needed for demo, but API endpoints should work)
- Smart mock AI search: fuzzy match on skills, name, location with weighted scoring + 500ms simulated delay

### Tech constraints:
- Motion v12 — import from `"motion/react"`
- SCSS Modules with camelCaseOnly
- Path alias `@/*` → `./src/*`
- Vite proxy to API on port 3001
- `@rearden/types` for shared types (extend if needed)

## Design the plan with:
1. New dependencies needed (react-router-dom, etc.)
2. Types to add/modify in packages/types
3. File structure for apps/web/src (components, pages, data, hooks, styles)
4. API routes to add in apps/api
5. Vite config changes (proxy)
6. Detailed component/page breakdown
7. Mock data structure
8. Search algorithm design
9. Animation strategy
10. Implementation order (what to build first, dependencies between steps)

Be specific about file paths, component names, and data flow. This will be used as an execution plan.

content.text: I'll start by thoroughly exploring the current codebase to understand the existing structure, configurations, and patterns before designing the implementation plan.

content.text: I have now thoroughly explored the entire codebase. Let me verify a few stock video sources to ensure the plan references real, working free video URLs.

input.query: Pexels free stock video CDN direct URL format 2026

content.content:
Web search results for query: "Pexels free stock video CDN direct URL format 2026"

Links: [{"title":"Free 4K Stock Videos & Full HD Video Clips to Download","url":"https://www.pexels.com/videos/"},{"title":"Best free stock video site of 2026: high quality production for less | TechRadar","url":"https://www.techradar.com/best/free-stock-videos"},{"title":"2026 Videos, Download The BEST Free 4k Stock Video Footage & 2026 HD Video Clips","url":"https://www.pexels.com/search/videos/2026/"},{"title":"Find free alternative to Pexels for developers - 2026","url":"https://freestuff.dev/alternative/pexels/"},{"title":"14 Best Free Stock Video Sites in 2026: The Ultimate Guide for Content Creators","url":"https://www.masrawysat111.com/2024/01/here-are-9-sites-to-download-free-cut.html"},{"title":"10 Best Free Stock Video Sites for 2026 (Safe & High Quality)","url":"https://swarmify.com/blog/10-best-free-stock-video-sites/"},{"title":"Pixabay API Documentation","url":"https://pixabay.com/api/docs/"},{"title":"15 Best Free Stock Photo Sites for Commercial Use 2026 - Life Guide","url":"https://www.yuanqilife.com/15-best-free-stock-photo-sites-for-commercial-use-2026/"},{"title":"Pexels Videos, Download The BEST Free 4k Stock Video Footage & Pexels HD Video Clips","url":"https://www.pexels.com/search/videos/pexels/"},{"title":"12 Stock Video Websites to Source Great Footage in 2024","url":"https://www.oberlo.com/blog/free-stock-video-websites"}]

Based on the search results, I found information about Pexels free stock videos, but the results don't contain specific details about direct CDN URL formats for accessing Pexels videos. 

Here's what I found:

Pexels Videos makes it easy to find free stock footage for your website, promo video or anything else, and all videos are free for personal and commercial use. Free stock videos are provided in MP4 format in HD, though the platform has limited clips in 4K.

The search results reference one example of a CDN-hosted video URL structure: "https://cdn.shopify.com/videos/c/o/v/96d73123a8cb45d4bea492af3a4ef545.mp4", though this appears to be from Shopify's CDN rather than Pexels' native CDN.

However, the search results don't provide the specific Pexels CDN direct URL format or API endpoints for accessing videos programmatically in 2026. For more detailed technical documentation about Pexels' video CDN URLs and API, you may need to check Pexels' official API documentation or developer documentation directly.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

input.query: AnimatePresence page transitions layout animations stagger children React components import from motion/react

content.text:
### Import AnimatePresence from Motion React

Source: https://motion.dev/docs/react-animate-presence

Shows the required import statement to use AnimatePresence component in a React application with Motion library.

```jsx
import { AnimatePresence } from "motion/react"
```

--------------------------------

### Troubleshooting Layout Animations with AnimatePresence and LayoutGroup (React)

Source: https://motion.dev/docs/react-animate-presence

When combining exit and layout animations, especially with `mode="sync"`, wrapping the animated group in `LayoutGroup` can resolve issues where components outside AnimatePresence don't correctly perform layout animations. This ensures proper coordination of layout changes.

```jsx
<LayoutGroup>
  <motion.ul layout>
    <AnimatePresence>
      {items.map(item => (
        <motion.li layout key={item.id} />
      ))}
    </AnimatePresence>
  </motion.ul>
</LayoutGroup>
```

--------------------------------

### AnimatePresence `popLayout` Mode for Reflowing Elements (React)

Source: https://motion.dev/docs/react-animate-presence

The `popLayout` mode in AnimatePresence causes exiting elements to be 'popped' out of the document flow, allowing surrounding elements to reflow immediately. This mode pairs effectively with the `layout` prop for smooth transitions of remaining elements. When using custom components as immediate children, they must be wrapped in `React.forwardRef`.

```jsx
<AnimatePresence>
  {items.map(item => (
    <motion.li layout exit={{ opacity: 0 }} />
  ))}
</AnimatePresence>
```

--------------------------------

### Disable Initial Animations with AnimatePresence `initial` Prop (React)

Source: https://motion.dev/docs/react-animate-presence

The `initial={false}` prop on AnimatePresence prevents any initial animations from running on children that are present when the component first renders. This is useful for controlling when animations should occur, typically only for mount/unmount transitions.

```jsx
<AnimatePresence initial={false}>
  <Slide key={activeItem.id} />
</AnimatePresence>
```

--------------------------------

### AnimatePresence custom Prop

Source: https://motion.dev/docs/react-animate-presence

Allows passing dynamic data to children's exit animations via variants, enabling more flexible animation control.

```APIDOC
## custom

### Description
When a component is removed, there's no longer a chance to update its props (because it's no longer in the React tree). Therefore we can't update its exit animation with the same render that removed the component.
By passing a value through `AnimatePresence`'s `custom` prop, we can use dynamic variants to change the `exit` animation.

### Method
Component Prop

### Endpoint
AnimatePresence

### Parameters
#### Request Body
- **custom** (any) - Optional - A value that can be accessed by children via `usePresenceData` to influence dynamic variants for exit animations.

### Request Example
```jsx
const variants = {
  hidden: (direction) => ({
    opacity: 0,
    x: direction === 1 ? -300 : 300
  }),
  visible: { opacity: 1, x: 0 }
}

export const Slideshow = ({ image, direction }) => (
  <AnimatePresence custom={direction}>
    <motion.img
      key={image.src}
      src={image.src}
      variants={variants}
      initial="hidden"
      animate="visible"
      exit="hidden"
    />
  </AnimatePresence>
)
```

### Response
#### Success Response (N/A)
- This prop controls component behavior, not a direct API response.

#### Response Example
N/A
```

Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the entire codebase and the relevant library APIs. Let me also quickly check Hono's file upload/serving capabilities since that's critical for the video upload feature.

content.text:
Now I have all the information needed. Let me compile the comprehensive implementation plan.

---

# Rearden Demo Web App -- Full Implementation Plan

## 1. New Dependencies

### `apps/web/package.json` -- add to `dependencies`:
- `react-router-dom` (v7.x) -- client-side routing with `createBrowserRouter`

No other new dependencies needed. Motion v12 (`motion`), React 19, SCSS, and all tooling are already in place.

### `apps/api/package.json` -- add to `dependencies`:
- No new dependencies needed. Hono already supports `parseBody()` for multipart uploads and `@hono/node-server` has `serveStatic`. The `node:fs` and `node:path` built-ins handle file I/O.

---

## 2. Types to Add/Modify in `packages/types`

### File: `C:\Users\yakik\Documents\projects\rearden\packages\types\src\candidate.ts`

Extend the existing `Candidate` interface with video and profile fields:

```ts
import type { User } from "./user";

export interface Candidate extends User {
  role: "candidate";
  resume: string | null;
  skills: string[];
  experience: number;
  // NEW fields
  videoUrl: string | null;       // URL to the uploaded video or stock CDN URL
  thumbnailUrl: string | null;   // poster/thumbnail for video player
  location: string;              // e.g. "San Francisco, CA"
  title: string;                 // e.g. "Senior Frontend Engineer"
  bio: string;                   // short self-description
  availability: "immediate" | "two_weeks" | "month" | "exploring";
}
```

### New file: `C:\Users\yakik\Documents\projects\rearden\packages\types\src\search.ts`

```ts
import type { Candidate } from "./candidate";

export interface SearchQuery {
  query: string;
  filters?: SearchFilters;
}

export interface SearchFilters {
  skills?: string[];
  location?: string;
  minExperience?: number;
  maxExperience?: number;
  availability?: string;
}

export interface SearchResult {
  candidate: Candidate;
  score: number;          // 0-100 relevance score
  matchedSkills: string[]; // which skills matched the query
  matchReason: string;     // human-readable "why this matched"
}

export interface SearchResponse {
  results: SearchResult[];
  query: string;
  totalCount: number;
}
```

### Update: `C:\Users\yakik\Documents\projects\rearden\packages\types\src\index.ts`

Add the new exports:

```ts
export type { User } from "./user";
export type { Candidate } from "./candidate";
export type { Recruiter } from "./recruiter";
export type { ApiResponse } from "./api-response";
export type { SearchQuery, SearchFilters, SearchResult, SearchResponse } from "./search";
```

---

## 3. File Structure for `apps/web/src`

```
apps/web/src/
  main.tsx                      # Entry -- mount RouterProvider
  App.tsx                       # DELETE current content, becomes root layout
  App.module.scss               # Root layout styles (dark theme shell)
  vite-env.d.ts                 # Already exists

  styles/
    global.scss                 # Reset + dark theme variables + typography
    _variables.scss             # SCSS variables: colors, spacing, breakpoints, z-index
    _mixins.scss                # Media query mixins, truncate, glass morphism, etc.
    _animations.scss            # Reusable keyframes (shimmer, pulse, glow)

  pages/
    Landing/
      Landing.tsx               # Hero, CTA, feature highlights
      Landing.module.scss
    Register/
      Register.tsx              # Candidate registration form + video upload
      Register.module.scss
    CandidateProfile/
      CandidateProfile.tsx      # Public profile with video player
      CandidateProfile.module.scss
    Search/
      Search.tsx                # THE MAIN PAGE -- recruiter search
      Search.module.scss

  components/
    Layout/
      Layout.tsx                # Shared shell: nav + Outlet + AnimatePresence
      Layout.module.scss
    Navbar/
      Navbar.tsx                # Top nav bar with logo + links
      Navbar.module.scss
    VideoPlayer/
      VideoPlayer.tsx           # Reusable <video> with poster, play/pause, controls
      VideoPlayer.module.scss
    VideoUpload/
      VideoUpload.tsx           # Drag-and-drop / click-to-upload with preview
      VideoUpload.module.scss
    CandidateCard/
      CandidateCard.tsx         # Card for search results: thumbnail, name, skills, score
      CandidateCard.module.scss
    SearchBar/
      SearchBar.tsx             # AI-style search input with animated border/glow
      SearchBar.module.scss
    SkillTag/
      SkillTag.tsx              # Pill/badge for a skill
      SkillTag.module.scss
    PageTransition/
      PageTransition.tsx        # Wrapper with motion enter/exit animations
      PageTransition.module.scss
    Button/
      Button.tsx                # Reusable button with variants (primary, secondary, ghost)
      Button.module.scss
    Input/
      Input.tsx                 # Styled form input with label + error
      Input.module.scss
    ScoreBadge/
      ScoreBadge.tsx            # Circular or bar score indicator (AI match %)
      ScoreBadge.module.scss
    Logo/
      Logo.tsx                  # Rearden logo/wordmark
      Logo.module.scss

  data/
    mockCandidates.ts           # 12 diverse mock candidates with stock video URLs
    stockVideos.ts              # Array of free Pexels/Pixabay CDN video URLs

  hooks/
    useSearch.ts                # Hook wrapping search API call with debounce + state
    useApi.ts                   # Generic fetch wrapper for API calls
    useVideoUpload.ts           # Hook for upload progress, preview, file handling

  lib/
    api.ts                      # Base API client (fetch wrapper, base URL, types)
    searchScoring.ts            # Client-side fuzzy search scoring (for instant preview)
    formatters.ts               # Utility: format experience, location, etc.

  router.tsx                    # createBrowserRouter definition + route tree
```

---

## 4. API Routes to Add in `apps/api`

### New file structure for API:
```
apps/api/src/
  index.ts                      # Already exists -- server bootstrap
  app.ts                        # Already exists -- extend with route mounts
  routes/
    candidates.ts               # CRUD for candidates
    search.ts                   # Search endpoint
    upload.ts                   # Video upload + serve
  data/
    mockCandidates.ts           # Server-side mock data (12 candidates)
  lib/
    searchEngine.ts             # Fuzzy scoring algorithm
```

### Endpoints:

**`GET /api/candidates`** -- List all candidates
- Returns: `ApiResponse<Candidate[]>`
- Source: mock data array

**`GET /api/candidates/:id`** -- Get single candidate
- Returns: `ApiResponse<Candidate>`
- 404 if not found

**`POST /api/candidates`** -- Create candidate (registration)
- Body: `{ name, email, skills[], location, title, bio, experience, availability }`
- Returns: `ApiResponse<Candidate>` with generated ID
- Adds to in-memory mock array (no DB for demo)

**`POST /api/search`** -- AI search
- Body: `SearchQuery` (`{ query, filters? }`)
- Returns: `ApiResponse<SearchResponse>`
- Simulated 500ms delay, then fuzzy scoring engine
- Weighted matching on skills, title, location, bio, name

**`POST /api/upload`** -- Video upload
- Multipart form: `file` field (video file)
- Saves to `apps/api/uploads/` directory
- Returns: `ApiResponse<{ url: string, filename: string }>`
- The returned `url` is `/api/uploads/<filename>`

**`GET /api/uploads/:filename`** -- Serve uploaded file
- Uses `serveStatic` or manual `fs.readFile` + stream
- Sets proper `Content-Type` headers (video/mp4, video/webm)

### Route registration in `apps/api/src/app.ts`:

```ts
import { Hono } from "hono";
import { logger } from "hono/logger";
import { cors } from "hono/cors";
import { candidatesRoutes } from "./routes/candidates.js";
import { searchRoutes } from "./routes/search.js";
import { uploadRoutes } from "./routes/upload.js";

export const app = new Hono();

app.use("*", logger());
app.use("*", cors());

// Existing routes
app.get("/", (c) => c.json({ success: true, data: { message: "Rearden API" } }));
app.get("/health", (c) => c.json({ success: true, data: { status: "ok" } }));

// Mount feature routes under /api prefix
app.route("/api/candidates", candidatesRoutes);
app.route("/api/search", searchRoutes);
app.route("/api", uploadRoutes);
```

---

## 5. Vite Config Changes

### File: `C:\Users\yakik\Documents\projects\rearden\apps\web\vite.config.ts`

Add the proxy configuration to forward `/api` requests to the API server on port 3001:

```ts
import { defineConfig } from "vite";
import react from "@vitejs/plugin-react";
import { resolve } from "path";

export default defineConfig({
  plugins: [react()],
  resolve: {
    alias: {
      "@": resolve(__dirname, "src"),
    },
  },
  css: {
    modules: {
      localsConvention: "camelCaseOnly",
    },
  },
  server: {
    port: 3000,
    proxy: {
      "/api": {
        target: "http://localhost:3001",
        changeOrigin: true,
      },
    },
  },
});
```

---

## 6. Detailed Component/Page Breakdown

### 6.1 Router (`apps/web/src/router.tsx`)

```tsx
import { createBrowserRouter } from "react-router-dom";
import { Layout } from "@/components/Layout/Layout";
import { Landing } from "@/pages/Landing/Landing";
import { Register } from "@/pages/Register/Register";
import { CandidateProfile } from "@/pages/CandidateProfile/CandidateProfile";
import { Search } from "@/pages/Search/Search";

export const router = createBrowserRouter([
  {
    path: "/",
    element: <Layout />,
    children: [
      { index: true, element: <Landing /> },
      { path: "register", element: <Register /> },
      { path: "candidate/:id", element: <CandidateProfile /> },
      { path: "search", element: <Search /> },
    ],
  },
]);
```

### 6.2 `main.tsx` (modified)

Replace the current `<App />` render with `<RouterProvider router={router} />`.

```tsx
import { StrictMode } from "react";
import { createRoot } from "react-dom/client";
import { RouterProvider } from "react-router-dom";
import { router } from "./router";
import "./styles/global.scss";

createRoot(document.getElementById("root")!).render(
  <StrictMode>
    <RouterProvider router={router} />
  </StrictMode>,
);
```

### 6.3 Layout Component

- Renders `<Navbar />` at top
- Wraps `<Outlet />` with `<AnimatePresence mode="wait">` for page transitions
- Uses a `<PageTransition>` wrapper keyed by `location.pathname`
- Dark background, max-width container

### 6.4 Navbar

- Logo "Rearden" on left (links to `/`)
- "For Candidates" link to `/register`
- "Search Talent" link to `/search`
- Subtle glass-morphism background effect
- Animated underline on active link (Motion `layoutId`)

### 6.5 Landing Page (`/`)

**Sections:**
1. **Hero** -- Large animated heading "Hire by watching, not reading." Subheading about video-first hiring. Two CTA buttons: "I'm a Candidate" (goes to `/register`) and "Find Talent" (goes to `/search`). Subtle background gradient animation.
2. **Feature highlights** -- 3 cards in a row: "Video Profiles", "AI-Powered Search", "Instant Insights". Each with icon, title, description. Cards stagger-animate on scroll into view.
3. **Preview** -- A floating mockup showing a candidate card with a video thumbnail to entice clicking through.

### 6.6 Register Page (`/register`)

**Form fields:**
- Name (text input)
- Email (text input)
- Title/Role (text input, e.g. "Frontend Engineer")
- Location (text input)
- Skills (comma-separated text input, parsed into array, or tag input)
- Years of experience (number input)
- Bio (textarea)
- Availability (select dropdown)
- Video Upload (VideoUpload component)

**Flow:**
1. User fills out form
2. If video is selected, upload it via `POST /api/upload` first, get back the URL
3. Submit full candidate data via `POST /api/candidates` with the `videoUrl`
4. On success, redirect to `/candidate/:id` to see their profile

### 6.7 Candidate Profile Page (`/candidate/:id`)

- Fetches candidate from `GET /api/candidates/:id`
- Large `<VideoPlayer>` at top with poster thumbnail
- Below: name, title, location, availability badge
- Skills displayed as `<SkillTag>` pills
- Experience years and bio section
- "Back to Search" link if coming from recruiter flow
- All sections animate in with staggered fade-up on mount

### 6.8 Search Page (`/search`) -- THE MAIN PAGE

**Layout:**
- Full-width `<SearchBar>` at the top with animated glowing border (premium AI feel)
- Placeholder text rotates: "Search for React developers in NYC...", "Find ML engineers with 5+ years...", etc.
- Below the search bar: result count + active filter chips
- Grid of `<CandidateCard>` components (2-3 columns responsive)
- On initial load (empty query): show all candidates with a "Featured Talent" heading

**SearchBar behavior:**
1. User types a query
2. 300ms debounce
3. Calls `POST /api/search` with `{ query }`
4. Shows loading shimmer on cards during fetch
5. Results replace grid with stagger animation

**CandidateCard:**
- Video thumbnail that auto-plays (muted) on hover with Motion scale effect
- Name, title, location
- Top 3 skill tags
- `<ScoreBadge>` showing AI match percentage (top-right corner)
- `matchReason` text (e.g. "Strong match: React, TypeScript, 6 years experience")
- Click navigates to `/candidate/:id`
- Cards animate in with `staggerChildren` from Motion

### 6.9 VideoPlayer Component

- Wraps `<video>` element
- Props: `src`, `poster`, `autoPlay?`, `muted?`, `controls?`, `className?`
- Handles loading state (shimmer placeholder)
- Custom play button overlay when paused (styled, not native)
- Rounded corners, subtle shadow

### 6.10 VideoUpload Component

- Drag-and-drop zone with dashed border
- Click to open file picker (`accept="video/*"`)
- Shows upload progress bar
- Once uploaded, shows inline video preview
- Props: `onUploadComplete(url: string)`, `onError(msg: string)`

### 6.11 Button Component

- Variants: `primary` (gradient accent), `secondary` (outlined), `ghost` (text-only)
- Sizes: `sm`, `md`, `lg`
- Motion `whileHover={{ scale: 1.02 }}` and `whileTap={{ scale: 0.98 }}`

### 6.12 PageTransition Component

```tsx
import { motion } from "motion/react";

export function PageTransition({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {
  return (
    <motion.div
      initial={{ opacity: 0, y: 20 }}
      animate={{ opacity: 1, y: 0 }}
      exit={{ opacity: 0, y: -20 }}
      transition={{ duration: 0.3, ease: "easeInOut" }}
    >
      {children}
    </motion.div>
  );
}
```

---

## 7. Mock Data Structure

### File: `apps/api/src/data/mockCandidates.ts`

12 diverse mock candidates. Each uses a free stock video from Pexels CDN. Example structure:

```ts
import type { Candidate } from "@rearden/types";

export const mockCandidates: Candidate[] = [
  {
    id: "c1",
    email: "sarah.chen@email.com",
    name: "Sarah Chen",
    role: "candidate",
    title: "Senior Frontend Engineer",
    location: "San Francisco, CA",
    skills: ["React", "TypeScript", "Next.js", "GraphQL", "CSS"],
    experience: 6,
    bio: "Passionate about building beautiful, accessible web experiences. Led the frontend team at a Series B startup...",
    resume: null,
    videoUrl: "https://videos.pexels.com/video-files/3252118/3252118-sd_640_360_25fps.mp4",
    thumbnailUrl: "https://images.pexels.com/videos/3252118/free-video-3252118.jpg?auto=compress&w=400",
    availability: "two_weeks",
    createdAt: "2025-03-01T10:00:00Z",
    updatedAt: "2025-03-01T10:00:00Z",
  },
  {
    id: "c2",
    email: "marcus.johnson@email.com",
    name: "Marcus Johnson",
    role: "candidate",
    title: "Full-Stack Developer",
    location: "Austin, TX",
    skills: ["Node.js", "Python", "React", "PostgreSQL", "AWS"],
    experience: 4,
    bio: "Full-stack developer with a background in cloud architecture...",
    resume: null,
    videoUrl: "https://videos.pexels.com/video-files/4065924/4065924-sd_640_360_25fps.mp4",
    thumbnailUrl: "https://images.pexels.com/videos/4065924/free-video-4065924.jpg?auto=compress&w=400",
    availability: "immediate",
    createdAt: "2025-03-02T10:00:00Z",
    updatedAt: "2025-03-02T10:00:00Z",
  },
  // ... 10 more candidates covering:
  // - ML/AI Engineer (Python, TensorFlow, PyTorch) -- New York
  // - DevOps Engineer (Kubernetes, Terraform, Docker) -- Remote
  // - iOS Developer (Swift, SwiftUI) -- Seattle
  // - Backend Engineer (Go, gRPC, Redis) -- Chicago
  // - Data Scientist (Python, R, SQL, Spark) -- Boston
  // - UX Designer / Frontend (Figma, React, CSS, A11y) -- LA
  // - Mobile Developer (React Native, Kotlin) -- Denver
  // - Blockchain Developer (Solidity, Rust, Web3) -- Miami
  // - QA/SDET Engineer (Playwright, Cypress, Jest) -- Portland
  // - Cloud Architect (AWS, Azure, Terraform) -- Remote
];
```

Key design decisions for mock data:
- Diverse names representing different backgrounds/genders
- Spread across US cities + 2 remote
- Skills overlap enough that searches feel intelligent (e.g. multiple people know React)
- Experience ranges from 2 to 12 years
- Availability mix across all options
- Stock video URLs from Pexels free video CDN (people talking to camera, professional setting videos)

The same data is duplicated on the frontend in `apps/web/src/data/mockCandidates.ts` for instant client-side fallback if the API is unreachable, but the primary flow goes through the API.

---

## 8. Search Algorithm Design

### File: `apps/api/src/lib/searchEngine.ts`

**Approach: Weighted fuzzy scoring with simulated AI delay**

```ts
interface ScoringWeights {
  skillExactMatch: 25;       // per skill that exactly matches
  skillPartialMatch: 15;     // per skill that partially matches (substring)
  titleMatch: 20;            // title contains query terms
  locationMatch: 15;         // location matches
  bioMatch: 5;               // bio contains query terms
  nameMatch: 10;             // name matches
  experienceBonus: 10;       // bonus for higher experience
}
```

**Algorithm steps:**

1. **Parse query**: Split the raw query string into tokens. Handle natural language like "React developer in San Francisco with 5 years experience"
   - Extract skill keywords (match against known skill list)
   - Extract location (match "in <location>" patterns)
   - Extract experience (match "N years" patterns)
   - Remaining tokens are general search terms

2. **Score each candidate** (0-100 scale):
   - For each parsed skill token, check against `candidate.skills`:
     - Exact match (case-insensitive): +25 points
     - Partial match (substring): +15 points
   - Title match: if any token appears in `candidate.title`: +20 points
   - Location match: if location token matches `candidate.location`: +15 points
   - Bio match: for each token found in `candidate.bio`: +5 points
   - Name match: if query includes candidate name: +10 points
   - Experience bonus: if candidate has > median experience: +10 points

3. **Normalize scores** to 0-100 range (divide by max possible score, multiply by 100)

4. **Generate matchReason**: Build a human-readable string like "Matched skills: React, TypeScript. Located in San Francisco."

5. **Sort by score descending**, filter out scores below 10 (threshold)

6. **Simulate delay**: `await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 500))` before returning

7. **Return**: `SearchResponse` with sorted results

**Empty query behavior**: Return all candidates sorted by `createdAt` with score 100 and reason "Featured talent".

---

## 9. Animation Strategy

All animations use Motion v12 imported from `"motion/react"`.

### Page Transitions
- `<AnimatePresence mode="wait">` wraps `<Outlet>` in Layout
- Each page wrapped in `<PageTransition>` keyed by `location.pathname`
- Enter: `opacity: 0, y: 20` -> `opacity: 1, y: 0` (300ms, easeOut)
- Exit: `opacity: 1, y: 0` -> `opacity: 0, y: -20` (200ms, easeIn)

### Landing Page
- Hero heading: `initial={{ opacity: 0, y: -30 }}` -> `animate={{ opacity: 1, y: 0 }}` with spring
- CTA buttons: stagger 100ms delay, scale from 0.9 to 1
- Feature cards: `whileInView` trigger, stagger 150ms, fade up from 40px

### Search Page -- Card Grid
- Container uses `staggerChildren: 0.06` in `variants`
- Each `<CandidateCard>` variant: `hidden: { opacity: 0, y: 30, scale: 0.95 }` -> `visible: { opacity: 1, y: 0, scale: 1 }`
- On query change: `AnimatePresence` exits old cards, enters new ones
- Loading state: cards show shimmer animation (CSS `@keyframes shimmer` with gradient)

### CandidateCard Hover
- `whileHover={{ y: -4, boxShadow: "0 20px 40px rgba(0,0,0,0.3)" }}`
- Video thumbnail: on hover, `scale: 1.05` with `transition: { duration: 0.3 }`
- Score badge: subtle pulse animation on appear

### SearchBar
- Animated glowing border: CSS custom property animated via Motion `animate` on the border color cycling through accent hues
- Focus state: border intensifies, subtle expand animation
- Placeholder text: crossfade between rotating suggestions using `AnimatePresence`

### Navbar
- Active link indicator: `motion.div` with `layoutId="nav-indicator"` for shared layout animation (the underline slides between links)

### Register Form
- Form fields stagger in on mount (50ms apart)
- Submit button: `whileTap={{ scale: 0.95 }}`
- Success redirect: whole form fades out before navigation

### Candidate Profile
- Video player: fades in with scale from 0.98 to 1
- Info sections below: stagger 100ms, fade up

## 10. Design / Dark Theme Specification

### SCSS Variables (`_variables.scss`)

```scss
// Colors -- dark premium palette
$bg-primary: #0a0a0f;         // near-black with blue tint
$bg-secondary: #12121a;       // card backgrounds
$bg-tertiary: #1a1a2e;        // elevated surfaces
$bg-glass: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.03); // glass morphism

$text-primary: #f0f0f5;       // almost white
$text-secondary: #8888a0;     // muted
$text-tertiary: #55556a;      // very muted

$accent-primary: #6c5ce7;     // purple
$accent-secondary: #00cec9;   // teal
$accent-gradient: linear-gradient(135deg, #6c5ce7, #00cec9);

$border-subtle: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.06);
$border-hover: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.12);

$glow-primary: 0 0 20px rgba(108, 92, 231, 0.3);
$glow-secondary: 0 0 20px rgba(0, 206, 201, 0.3);

// Spacing
$space-xs: 0.25rem;
$space-sm: 0.5rem;
$space-md: 1rem;
$space-lg: 1.5rem;
$space-xl: 2rem;
$space-2xl: 3rem;
$space-3xl: 4rem;

// Breakpoints
$bp-mobile: 480px;
$bp-tablet: 768px;
$bp-desktop: 1024px;
$bp-wide: 1280px;

// Border radius
$radius-sm: 6px;
$radius-md: 10px;
$radius-lg: 16px;
$radius-xl: 24px;
$radius-full: 9999px;

// Typography
$font-sans: 'Inter', system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif;
$font-mono: 'JetBrains Mono', 'Fira Code', monospace;
```

### Key design patterns:
- **Glass morphism** for cards: `backdrop-filter: blur(12px)`, semi-transparent backgrounds
- **Gradient accents** on buttons and active states
- **Subtle borders** that brighten on hover
- **Generous whitespace** -- not cramped
- **Rounded corners** -- `$radius-lg` for cards, `$radius-full` for pills/tags
- **Shadows** use colored tints, not gray (purple glow for primary elements)
- Add Inter font via Google Fonts in `index.html`

**Step 5.3** -- Edge cases

**Step 5.4** -- Performance

## Key Architectural Decisions

1. **No real DB for demo**: Mock data lives in-memory in `apps/api/src/data/mockCandidates.ts`. New registrations are pushed to the array (lost on restart). This keeps the demo zero-config.

2. **API-first even for mock data**: All data flows through the API, not hardcoded in the frontend. This makes the architecture realistic and easy to swap in a real DB later.

3. **Client-side data copy as fallback**: `apps/web/src/data/mockCandidates.ts` mirrors the server data for instant loading if needed, but the primary path is always API calls.

4. **Video strategy**: Mock candidates use Pexels CDN URLs (no local files). Real uploads via the registration form go to `apps/api/uploads/`. Both paths result in a URL that the `<VideoPlayer>` component can play.

5. **Search is server-side**: Even though it is mock, the search runs on the API to simulate a real AI endpoint. The 500ms delay makes it feel like a real AI inference call.

6. **SCSS Modules with camelCaseOnly**: Every component gets its own `.module.scss` file. Class names are accessed as `styles.someClass` (camelCase). No BEM needed -- the module scoping handles isolation.

7. **Motion v12 from `"motion/react"`**: All animation imports use the new Motion package, not the legacy `framer-motion`.
